commands.name: claudient context-lens
commands.description: Analyzes the active Claude Code session to show exactly what context Claude is seeing — files loaded, memory state, hooks active, MCP connections.

options.description: Show detailed breakdowns of each context layer (system prompt, memory, file state, tool availability)
Output in JSON format for programmatic consumption
Show token count per context component
Suggest ways to reduce context bloat and improve efficiency

examples.description: Export full context state for analysis

commands.sampleOutput:
claudient context-lens — Session Context Analysis
────────────────────────────────────────────
Session: claude-code-2026-06-22
Model: claude-haiku-4-5-20251001
Tokens used this session: 42,340 / 200,000

Context Layers (58,240 tokens total):
  1. System Prompt              12,400 tokens (21%)
     • Core Claude Code harness
     • Tool definitions (41 tools)
     • Safety guidelines
  
  2. Project Memory             8,600 tokens (15%)
     • CLAUDE.md (Claudient rules)
     • MEMORY.md (persistent state)
     • .claude/settings.json
  
  3. Loaded Files               18,900 tokens (32%)
     • site/src/components/os/apps/CliApp.tsx  (8,240 tokens)
     • plugins/claudient-commands/commands/git/ (6,100 tokens)
     • .claude-plugin/marketplace.json (4,560 tokens)
  
  4. Active Hooks               3,200 tokens (5%)
     • caveman-mode.md (token optimizer)
     • project-rules.md (build conventions)
     • security-reviewer hook
  
  5. MCP Connections            2,800 tokens (5%)
     • postgres (pg-mcp-server)
     • github (github-mcp-server)
     • stripe (payments MCP)
  
  6. Task List                  4,100 tokens (7%)
     • 3 active tasks in memory
     • Dependencies tracked
  
  7. Tool Allowlist             6,240 tokens (11%)
     • 32 whitelisted bash patterns
     • 8 whitelisted MCP operations
     • Git operations permitted
────────────────────────────────────────────
Recommendations:
  • 4 files loaded that aren't referenced — consider unloading
  • Task #1 is stale (>30 min) — mark completed or reset
  • Caveman mode active: saving ~1,200 tokens/response
  • Consider archiving MEMORY.md to a separate session

→ Run: claudient context-lens --optimize

commands.name: claudient vault
commands.description: Encrypts/decrypts secrets in .claude/settings.local.json and generates secure vaults for multi-team environments. Auto-rotates keys, audits access logs.

options.description: Encrypt all secrets in settings.local.json using AES-256
Decrypt vault and temporarily expose secrets (one session only)
Rotate all encryption keys and re-encrypt existing secrets
Set time-to-live for decrypted secrets (default: 1 hour)
Create org-wide vault shared across team members
Show access logs and who accessed secrets when

examples.description: Rotate keys for entire team and re-encrypt

commands.sampleOutput:
claudient vault — Secret Management
────────────────────────────────────────────
Action: encrypt

Scanning: .claude/settings.local.json
  ✓ Found 8 secrets:

    • GITHUB_TOKEN

    • AWS_SECRET_ACCESS_KEY

  ✓ Generated master key: vault-key-2026-06-22
  ✓ Encrypted 8 secrets
  ✓ Backed up original: .claude/settings.local.json.bak
  ✓ Secured .claude/settings.local.json (chmod 600)

Vault Summary:
  Status: ENCRYPTED
  Secrets: 8/8 encrypted
  Key rotation: due 2026-08-22 (90 days)
  Last accessed: 2026-06-22 10:15 UTC

  Secrets will auto-lock in 1 hour

commands.name: claudient mesh
commands.description: Multi-agent orchestrator — coordinates parallel Claude subagents to divide-and-conquer large codebases. Automatically partitions work, merges results, resolves conflicts.

options.description: Maximum number of parallel agents (default: 4, max: 16)
Partitioning strategy: file-based, module-based, domain-based, or load-balanced (default: file-based)
Max token spend across all agents (e.g., --budget 500000 for $15)
Conflict resolution: first-write, latest-write, consensus, manual (default: consensus)
Save progress snapshots every N agents complete (enables resume on failure)
Show partition plan without executing agents

examples.command: claudient mesh 'Refactor all utils/ to TypeScript' --max-agents 6
claudient mesh 'Add logging to all API handlers' --strategy module-based --checkpoint 2
examples.description: Partition by module, save checkpoint every 2 agents

examples.command: claudient mesh 'Migrate ORM from Sequelize to Prisma' --dry-run
examples.description: See how task will be divided before running

commands.sampleOutput:
